articles.title: The AI Cold War Begins | US, China & Why India Needs Sovereign AI
articles.description: The battle for AI supremacy is no longer just about building better models. The US is restricting access to advanced AI systems while China is blocking AI talent and technology from leaving its borders. What does this mean for India and the future of AI sovereignty? Tech Today breaks down the new AI Cold War.

articles.title: Watch | TN CM Vijay takes bus ride after launching 300 new govt buses
articles.description: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay: Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay flagged off 300 new government buses before taking a ride on one of them, interacting with the conductor and buying a ticket. The new fleet includes diesel and BS-VI CNG buses to strengthen public transport.

articles.title: EPFO Claims Portal To Go Offline From Tonight For Three Days: Here's What Subscribers Need To Know
articles.description: Online claim submission and processing will remain unavailable from 12:00 am on June 26 until 11:59 pm on June 28, 2026.

articles.title: DoT opens applications under new telecom authorisation regime
articles.description: The Department of Telecom has opened applications for companies seeking authorization to provide telecommunication services under the new Telecom Act, 2023. Existing licensees can also migrate to this new regime. A digital portal is now live for submissions. This shift replaces the previous contractual license system. Existing players migrating will need to pay an entry fee and relinquish overlapping licenses. Bank guarantees have also been simplified for new applicants.

articles.title: $48 billion investment, AI expansion by 2030: Amazon CEO shares big plans for India
articles.description: The Amazon CEO further stated that since 2010, the tech company has invested $40 billion into India.

articles.title: India leads APAC in AI agent interest, with 60% consumers keen: Report
articles.description: A new Adobe report finds India is the most receptive market for agentic AI in APAC, with 60% of consumers interested in creating a personal AI agent. Over half are willing to engage with AI-powered experiences and brand agents.

articles.title: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announces plans to invest additional $13 bn in India
articles.description: Fresh investment will expand AI, cloud, e-commerce and logistics infrastructure, taking Amazon's cumulative investment commitment in India to more than $88 billion by 2030

articles.title: TVS Starts Production of India-Made Norton Atlas Middleweight ADV Bike at Hosur Plant
articles.description: TVS Motor Company has started production of the Norton Atlas at its Hosur facility with the first unit completing assembly prior to its global launch next month. This move establishes India as the main manufacturing hub for Norton bikes intended for international markets.

articles.title: Hong Kong turns to AI to tackle persistent rat infestations, but challenges remain
articles.description: Experts say the city’s rat problem remains an environmental management challenge that artificial intelligence can help monitor, but not solve on its own.

articles.description: Air India has launched its first Easy Connect flight from Varanasi, allowing international passengers to complete check-in, customs and immigration at their home airport.

articles.title: Land Rover Classic unveils colour-changing Defender V8: What’s special
articles.description: Powering the Classic Defender V8 is a naturally aspirated 5.0-litre V8 petrol engine, delivering 405 PS of power and 515 Nm of torque. The engine comes paired with an eight-speed ZF automatic transmission. The company says that the Classic Defender has also comprehensively re-engineered the chassis to match the increased performance.

articles.title: Bengaluru techie reveals why she is still excited to walk into Microsoft office after 5 years, and it's not about perks
articles.description: A Microsoft employee in Bengaluru, Deepika Koul, has captured social media attention for her enduring enthusiasm at work after five years. She attributes her sustained excitement not to the company's name, but to a supportive workplace culture fostering collaboration and personal growth. Koul highlights how her colleagues' encouragement and meaningful interactions positively impact her mental wellbeing, urging others to value such environments.

articles.title: Nothing Phone 4b design revealed ahead of July 7 launch: Know what it offers
articles.description: Nothing today has unveiled the design of Phone 4b, which is said to be “the first device in a brand-new series.”

articles.title: NHL Trade Rumors: Maple Leafs’ $46.5M winger suddenly becomes one of the hottest names available
articles.description: Matthew Knies is the name everyone in the NHL is talking about this offseason. The Toronto Maple Leafs have not committed to trading him, but the conversations are real and the competition is fierce. Buffalo holds the most leverage with the fourth-overall pick. San Jose sees him as the ideal running mate for Macklin Celebrini. Montreal has history here and genuine need. How this plays out could reshape at least one of these franchises heading into next season.

articles.title: ASTS Stock Rises Overnight: Rakuten’s AST SpaceMobile Launch Plan Gets Japan Nod To Take On SpaceX’s Starlink
articles.description: Rakuten is turning to AST SpaceMobile as it lacks access to the 2GHz band used by SpaceX’s Starlink.

articles.title: All you need to know about Meta’s Instagram, Whatsapp and Facebook subscription plans in India
articles.description: Meta has launched paid subscription plans for WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook in India, offering customisation and convenience features for a monthly fee. WhatsApp Plus costs Rs 79, while Instagram and Facebook Plus are Rs 99 (with an introductory Rs 49 for Instagram). These moves aim to diversify revenue beyond advertising and fund AI investments. The success of these subscriptions in India, where users are price-sensitive, remains to be seen.

articles.title: Daily Pisces Horoscope: Astrologer warns against haste and risk; follow gentle routines
articles.description: Today, Pisces, navigate with care as the stars suggest a tendency to rush and misunderstand situations. Pay special attention while engaging in physical activities or driving to avoid mishaps. In your relationships, seek to preserve peace rather than engage in conflicts. Financially, be deliberate in your choices and prioritize essential expenses to maintain stability, while also being mindful of your health and well-being amid potential stress.

articles.description: The new Siri app on iOS 27 defaults to the new Siri AI, but there is a way to quickly switch to ChatGPT at any time. Here’s how it works.

articles.title: Mosyle launches new service to help parents manage Mac and iPad screen time for K-12 devices at home
articles.description: Managing school-issued Macs and iPads has always been a balancing act between IT control and parent visibility when the devices go home. Today, Mosyle has announced Mosyle@Home , aimed at solving this problem. This new platform gives parents and guardians an official way to manage school devices after hours, and it supports school-issued iPads and Macs.

articles.title: Daily Aries Horoscope: Partner support, money caution; focus on studies and health
articles.description: Aries, today is a great day for partnerships, as the Moon encourages teamwork and collaboration. Venus adds a touch of warmth to your daily interactions, highlighting the importance of kind gestures over elaborate plans. Be cautious with your words, particularly towards women, due to Saturn's influence. Expect positive shifts in love and career through cooperative efforts and fresh opportunities. Financially, prioritize sensible savings over risky investments.

articles.title: iPhone 17 set to break a record no flagship has touched in 15 years
articles.description: When Apple released the iPhone 17 in September, the timer started for the new flagship model breaking a record set 15 years ago. The record will take more than a year to break, but based on current rumors, the iPhone 17 will be the first to steal the crown.

articles.title: India rewrites telecom rulebook, replacing licence contracts with statutory authorisations
articles.description: New government rules replace the telecom industry's contractual licensing with a unilateral statutory model. Operators will now receive authorization after meeting requirements, with future government directives becoming binding terms. Violations will be treated as statutory breaches, managed internally, and can be appealed to the TDSAT. This shift aims to streamline regulation, though telcos expressed concerns about investment uncertainty.

articles.title: Democracy hits the doorstep: Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls in Ludhiana district from today.
articles.description: Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls begins in Ludhiana: officials to verify 26.84 lakh voters via home visits until July 24; draft rolls Aug 3, final Oct 1.

articles.title: Here's when Apple is starting mass production of the iPhone Ultra
articles.description: Recently there's been some controversy about the iPhone Ultra's release window, with a report claiming it was delayed, which was then immediately refuted by another source, and then another one. Today we get one more 'confirmation' that the iPhone Ultra will be unveiled as scheduled, in September, alongside the iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max. The issues relating to the hinge have allegedly been resolved, and the mass production of the iPhone Ultra will be starting in late July. This information comes from a report in Korean media, purportedly originating from "Apple supply chain...

articles.title: China's EV exports in May rise 50% on-year to reach record high
articles.description: Chinas exports of electric vehicles hit a record $9.2 billion in May, rising nearly 50% year-on-year, as global demand for cleaner transport strengthened amid higher oil prices linked to the Iran conflict.
